title: Why You Should Cut Sarcasm from Your Life
subtitle: 别再用讽刺伪装幽默：它对关系的伤害超乎想象

paragraphs.en: Sarcasm and jazz have something surprisingly in common: You know them when you hear them. Sarcasm is mostly understood through tone of voice, which is used to portray the opposite of the literal words. For example, when someone says, "Well, that's exactly what I need right now," their tone can tell you it's not what they need at all.
paragraphs.zh: 讽刺和爵士乐有个惊人的共同点：你一听便知。讽刺主要通过语气来传达，语气被用来表达与字面意思相反的含义。例如，当有人说“嗯，这正是我现在需要的”时，其语气会告诉你这根本就不是他需要的。

paragraphs.en: Most frequently, sarcasm highlights an irritation or is, quite simply, mean. If you want to be happier and improve your relationships, cut out sarcasm. Why? Because sarcasm is actually hostility disguised as humor.
paragraphs.zh: 最常见的情况是，讽刺凸显了恼怒，或者干脆就是刻薄。如果你想变得更快乐并改善人际关系，就别再说讽刺的话了。为什么？因为讽刺实际上是伪装成幽默的敌意。

paragraphs.en: Despite smiling outwardly, many people who receive sarcastic comments feel put down and often think the sarcastic person is rude or contemptible. Indeed, the origin of the word sarcasm derives from the Greek word "sarkazein" which literally means "to tear or strip the flesh off." Hence, it's no wonder that sarcasm is often preceded by the word "cutting" and that it hurts.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管表面上保持微笑，但许多接收到讽刺评论的人都会感到被贬低，并常常认为讽刺的人粗鲁或可鄙。事实上，“讽刺”一词源于希腊语“sarkazein”，字面意思是“撕下或剥掉肉”。因此，难怪讽刺常被冠以“尖刻的”一词，而且它确实伤人。

paragraphs.en: What's more, since actions strongly determine thoughts and feelings, when a person consistently acts sarcastically it may only serve to heighten their underlying hostility and insecurity. After all, sarcasm can be used as a subtle form of bullying — and most bullies are angry, insecure, or cowardly.
paragraphs.zh: 更重要的是，既然行为在很大程度上决定思想和情感，那么一个人持续表现讽刺，可能只会加剧其潜在的敌意和不安全感。毕竟，讽刺可以被用作一种隐蔽的欺凌形式——而大多数欺凌者都是愤怒、缺乏安全感或懦弱的。

paragraphs.en: Alternatively, when a person stops voicing negative comments, especially sarcastic ones, they may soon start to feel happier and more self-confident. Also, other people in their life benefit even more because they no longer have to hear the emotionally hurtful language. Now, I'm not saying all sarcasm is bad.
paragraphs.zh: 相反，当一个人停止发表负面评论，尤其是讽刺性评论时，他们可能很快就会开始感到更快乐、更自信。此外，他们生活中的其他人受益更大，因为他们无需再听到那些伤感情的话。当然，我并不是说所有讽刺都是坏的。

paragraphs.en: It may just be better used sparingly — like a potent spice in cooking. Too much of the spice, and the dish will be overwhelmed by it. Similarly, an occasional dash of sarcastic wit can spice up a chat and add an element of humor to it.
paragraphs.zh: 也许少量使用讽刺更好——就像烹饪中的强力香料一样。香料太多，菜肴就会被其味道淹没。同样，偶尔来点讽刺的俏皮话可以为聊天增色，并增添一丝幽默。

paragraphs.en: But a big or steady serving of sarcasm will overwhelm the emotional flavor of any conversation and can taste very bitter to its recipient. So, tone down the sarcasm and work on clever wit instead, which is usually without any hostility and thus more appreciated. In essence, sarcasm is easy while true, harmless wit takes talent.
paragraphs.zh: 但大量或持续不断的讽刺会掩盖任何对话的情感风味，给接受者留下极其苦涩的余味。因此，请减少讽刺，转而修炼巧妙的机智。机智通常不含敌意，因而更受交流对象的欢迎。本质上，讽刺很容易，而真正无害的机智则需要天赋。

paragraphs.en: Thus, the main difference between wit and sarcasm is that sarcasm is often hostility disguised as humor. Witty statements are usually in response to someone's unhelpful remarks, and the intent is to untangle and clarify by emphasizing absurdities. Sarcastic statements are expressed in a cutting manner; witty remarks are delivered with undisguised and harmless humor.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，机智与讽刺的主要区别在于，讽刺往往是伪装成幽默的敌意。诙谐的言论通常是对他人无益言论的回应，其意图是通过强调其荒谬之处来理清和阐明问题。讽刺性的言论以尖刻的方式表达；而诙谐的言论则通过毫不掩饰且无伤大雅的幽默来传达。
